name: Suburban Sky
milkyWayVisibility: Marginal — Milky Way visible overhead only on best nights; washed out near horizon
nearestDarkSkyReserves: South Downs National Park Dark Sky Reserve
description: Typical suburban skies. The Milky Way is only marginally visible overhead on the clearest nights and is invisible near the horizon. Only the brightest deep-sky objects can be detected with the naked eye. Light pollution creates a noticeable overall sky glow, and clouds are noticeably brighter than the sky background. This is the most common sky quality for suburban residents in developed countries. About 30% of the global population lives under Class 5 skies. The naked-eye star count drops from thousands in dark skies to roughly 500-1000 visible stars.
